title: Binary Search on Rotated Array with Repeated Elements and Index Constraint

statement.markdown:
Given a rotated sorted array with repeated elements, find the index of a target element in the array within a specified range.

The array is rotated by an unknown number of positions and may contain duplicate elements.

Write a function to find the target element within the specified range.

For example, if the input array is `[4, 5, 6, 7, 0, 1, 2]` and the target element is `0`, the output should be `3` because `0` appears at index `3` in the array.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an array of integers and two integers representing the start and end indices of the target range. The input array will have at least one element.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be the index of the target element within the specified range.
statement.notes:
Time complexity: O(log n)
Space complexity: O(1)

examples.explanation: Target element 0 appears at index 3 in the array.
Target element 2 appears at index 5 in the array.

hints.text: Use a modified binary search algorithm to search within the specified range.

editorial.title: Binary Search on Rotated Array with Repeated Elements and Index Constraint
editorial.intuition: The key idea is to use a modified binary search algorithm that searches within the specified range.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve iterating over the entire array and checking each element within the specified range.
editorial.betterSolution: The better solution uses a binary search approach to search within the specified range.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses a binary search approach with an additional check to handle repeated elements within the specified range.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the binary search approach correctly finds the target element within the specified range.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the optimal solution involves testing it with example inputs to verify its correctness.
commonMistakes: Not searching within the specified range
